std-confirmation.name: std-confirmation
std-confirmation.description: Modal-only confirmation trait: renders ONLY to the `modal` slot. Consumers configure icon/title/alertMessage/confirmLabel at the trait-ref call site via `config: { ... }`. Side effects (persist delete, notify, etc.) belong in a sibling coordinator trait that `listens { ConfirmTrait CONFIRM -> DO_DELETE }`.

std-display.name: std-display
std-display.description:
std-display — DEPRECATED. The earlier shape inlined a hard-coded
chart + graph-view + several stat cards into a single render-ui spec
with @entity.X bindings that no transition explicitly set, which
violates the explicit-binding rule (gap #25 in
docs/Almadar_Std_Verification.md). The chart payload was hand-
authored literal data, not aggregated from a real entity stream.

Use these instead:
  std-stats   — embeddable stat-card aggregations (count / sum / avg
                / min / max / distinct), ITEMS_LOADED listener.
  std-graphs  — embeddable charts (bar / line / pie / area / donut),
                config-driven categoryField + aggregation, same
                ITEMS_LOADED listener shape.

Compose them in a molecule alongside std-browse the same way
std-filtered-list composes std-search / std-filter / std-browse /
std-pagination. See std-dashboard.lolo for the canonical pattern.

This file is preserved (and still embedded) so existing projects
that import `std/behaviors/std-display` keep loading; new dashboards
should target std-stats + std-graphs.

std-drawer.name: std-drawer
std-drawer.description: core atom — std-drawer (states: closed, error, loading, open)

std-filter.name: std-filter
std-filter.description:
std-filter as a Function — single primitive: filter-group. The atom's
own FilterTarget entity is the UI-state container — every FILTER
transition persists field/value into the entity. Visual reflection of
the persisted value (filter-group `value` prop) is a follow-up that
needs the pattern registry + UI component to accept a `value` prop;
the persistence side is done.

std-gallery.name: std-gallery
std-gallery.description: core atom — std-gallery (states: browsing, error, loading, viewing)

std-graphs.name: std-graphs
std-graphs.description:
std-graphs — embeddable chart atom. Drops into a dashboard molecule
alongside std-stats / std-browse; wire its ITEMS_LOADED listener to
the upstream collection's success emit, then configure `chartType` +
`categoryField` + (`valueField` | `aggregation`) per call-site to
declare WHAT to chart.

Aggregation runs in the lolo state machine: ITEMS_LOADED groups
@payload.data by @config.categoryField (array/groupBy returns
{<category>: [<rows>]}) then array/maps over (object/entries result)
to apply the configured aggregation (count | sum | avg | min | max)
per bucket. The resulting chart-data array of {label, value} drops
into the existing chart pattern with no aggregator component required.

The `ChartPoint` type alias narrows the entity field to the exact
shape the `chart` pattern's `data` prop expects (`{label, value}`),
so no `Record<string, unknown>` cast lands at the call site —
schema authors keep strict typing end-to-end.

std-modal.name: std-modal
std-modal.description: Modal-only trait: renders ONLY to the `modal` slot. The parent molecule's layout/browse trait is responsible for the `main` slot (the list, the trigger buttons, etc.). Consumers configure icon/title/fields/mode at the trait-ref call site via `config: { ... }`. Side effects (persist, notify) belong in a sibling coordinator trait that `listens { ModalTrait SAVE -> DO_PERSIST }`.

std-pagination.name: std-pagination
std-pagination.description:
std-pagination as a Function — single primitive: pagination control.
The Pagination UI component emits `{page}` for page changes; the atom's
PAGE event payload matches that shape. ITEMS_LOADED accepts an upstream
data-loaded event payload `{data : [object]}` and derives totalPages =
ceil(len(data) / pageSize). Molecules wire e.g.
`FilteredItemBrowse.BrowseItemLoaded -> ITEMS_LOADED` to keep the
pagination control in lockstep with the data hub. The PagedItem entity
persists currentPage and the derived totalPages so reads from
@entity.totalPages reflect the latest row count.

std-push.name: std-push
std-push.description: External-event subscription atom. Wraps os/watch-http with a small lifecycle state machine (idle -> subscribed ↔ reconnecting) using the emit: config to turn incoming messages + failures into closed-circuit events on the orbital's bus. Intra-frame: embed @trait.PushIndicator in a layout to show a live connection status pill (see molecule std-push-activity-log). Inter-frame: any trait can listens: PUSH_RECEIVED and react to payloads.

std-related.name: std-related
std-related.description: Relation-rendering atom. Assumes the linked entity's relation field has already been hydrated by an upstream fetch with include; this atom only owns the rendering of the related children. Paired with std-browse via @trait.X this gives a one-line master-detail layout: hstack [ @trait.ParentList, @trait.ParentRelated ]. The molecule std-related-master-detail is the canonical showcase.

std-search.name: std-search
std-search.description:
std-search as a Function — single primitive: search-input. The atom's
own SearchResult entity is the UI-state container — SEARCH persists
@payload.searchTerm (matching the SearchInput component's emit shape)
into @entity.searchTerm. Cross-trait listeners route the same payload
field to data atoms (Browse REFETCH_QUERY).

std-selection.name: std-selection
std-selection.description: core atom — std-selection (states: error, idle, loading, selected, selecting)

std-stats.name: std-stats
std-stats.description:
std-stats — embeddable analytics-card atom. Drop into a dashboard
molecule alongside std-browse / std-search / std-filter; wire its
ITEMS_LOADED listener to the upstream collection's success emit, then
configure `metrics` per call-site to declare WHICH aggregations to
show.

Aggregation runs in the lolo state machine (no custom React component
beyond stat-display): the ITEMS_LOADED transition uses array/map over
@config.metrics, applies the per-metric `filter` predicate to
@payload.data, then dispatches the chosen aggregation kind. Cards
drop into a data-list whose renderItem maps each card to a
stat-display pattern. The atom owns an auxiliary entity (StatsItem)
for UI state — explicit-binding rule applies: every @entity.X is set
explicitly via (set @entity.X ...) on each transition.

Each entry in `metrics` (all but `label` optional):
- label       string!                          card title
- aggregation count|sum|avg|min|max  default count   what to compute
- field       string                           numeric entity field for sum/avg/min/max
- filter      SExpression                      row predicate (e.g. fn-form testing @row.status). Drops non-matching
                                               rows BEFORE aggregation. Omitted = all rows. Compiler statically
                                               unrolls array/map over @config.metrics so the fn-form is fully
                                               specialized into pure TS at codegen time.
- format      number|currency|percent default number   value formatting
- variant     default|primary|success|warning|error|info   color
- icon        string                                       lucide icon name
- prefix      string                                       prepended to displayed value (e.g. "≈ ")
- suffix      string                                       appended (e.g. " /mo", " ms")
- max         number                                       optional denominator. >0 renders "value / max".
- target      number                                       optional progress target. >0 renders a progress bar.
- trend       number                                       signed delta vs previous period. >0 ↑ green, <0 ↓ red.

For dynamic max/target/trend (computed against @payload.data),
schema authors today must precompute and pass literal numbers; an
SExpression-evaluating slot is a future extension.

std-tabs.name: std-tabs
std-tabs.description:
std-tabs — embeddable tab-navigation atom. Drop into a layout
molecule to give the user a tabs strip that swaps which trait fills
the active tab's slot. Each tab points at a sibling trait via a
@trait.X content reference; clicking a tab updates the atom's own
@entity.activeTab and re-renders so only the matching trait's
output shows. Events remap at the call site (TAB_CHANGED -> Y) so
the host molecule can listen for routing, persistence, analytics.

Each entry in `tabs` (all but `id` and `label` optional):
- id      string!  stable tab id (used as activeTab key)
- label   string!  visible tab text
- icon    string   lucide icon name
- badge   string   small badge in the tab strip (e.g. unread count)
- content string   @trait.X reference for the panel body. The host
                   molecule binds a sibling trait there; only the
                   active tab's content renders at any time. Omit
                   to leave the panel empty.

Config:
- tabs        [TabSpec] = []
- defaultTab  string = ''                       id of the initial active tab.
                                                Falls back to tabs[0].id if blank.
- variant     default|pills|underline = default visual style
- orientation horizontal|vertical = horizontal  tab strip orientation

Compiler statically unrolls array/map over @config.tabs so every
TabSpec field is fully baked into the emitted props at codegen
time.

std-cart.name: std-cart
std-cart.description: Daisy-chain composition showcase (no `on EVENT` overrides). Modal + Confirmation trait refs are configured via `config: { ... }` at the call site. Side effects (persist) live in sibling coordinator traits that listen for the modal/confirmation trait's emits.

std-dashboard.name: std-dashboard
std-dashboard.description:
std-dashboard — molecule composing std-stats + std-graphs + std-browse
into a typical dashboard view: stat cards across the top, two charts
in the middle, data list at the bottom. Browse is the data hub — it
fetches the rows, emits BrowseItemLoaded; Stats and Graphs both listen
for it via cross-trait `Browse.BrowseItemLoaded -> ITEMS_LOADED`. All
three atoms render into the layout's `main` slot via `@trait.X`
placement.

std-filtered-list.name: std-filtered-list
std-filtered-list.description: std-filtered-list — molecule composing slim Search/Filter/Browse/Pagination atoms

std-list.name: std-list
std-list.description: Daisy-chain composition showcase: no `on EVENT { ... }` trait-ref overrides. Modal + Confirmation trait refs are configured via `config: { ... }` at the call site. Side effects (persist) live in a sibling coordinator trait (`ListItemPersistor`) that listens for the modal/confirmation trait emits. Mirrors std-cart's structure. std-list omits the read-only VIEW path that the old version had — form-section's mode set only supports "create" | "edit", and a dedicated detail-view pattern is tracked separately (std-detail).
